fresh_facts.claim: U.S.-Israeli strikes killed IRGC intelligence chief Maj. Gen. Majid Khademi and at least 25 others in Iran on Monday, April 7, 2026.
fresh_facts.source: Sepah News / Israeli Defense Ministry statement

fresh_facts.claim: President Trump issued a deadline of Tuesday 8 PM Washington time (April 8, 00:00 UTC) for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face U.S. strikes on infrastructure.
Israeli strikes hit Tehran early Tuesday local time, targeting IRGC sites, missile facilities, and petrochemical plants, with explosions reported near Azadi Square and Sharif University.
Iran launched 14 missiles and 19 drones at UAE, 7 ballistic missiles at Saudi Arabia, and missiles at Israel and Jordan in the past 24 hours, causing 4 deaths in Israel and 2 in Jordan.
IRGC vowed 'Operation Crushing Revenge' for Maj. Gen. Khademi's death, targeting planners of the 'crime'.
The heaviest wave of U.S.-Israeli strikes on Monday killed 49 civilians and injured 58, according to HRANA report.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed in U.S.-Israeli strikes, and his son Mojtaba Khamenei was appointed as his successor by the Assembly of Experts.
Mojtaba Khamenei issued a statement as new Supreme Leader demanding adversaries' security be removed.
Iran appointed Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, a hardline ex-IRGC deputy commander, as new Supreme National Security Council chief, replacing Ali Larijani who was killed in an Israeli strike.
Iran rejected a 45-day ceasefire proposal via Pakistan and U.S. efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, demanding a permanent end to conflict instead.

Trump announced that U.S. envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner held productive phone talks with a 'top' Iranian official and instructed the Pentagon to postpone attacks on Iranian energy infrastructure for five days.
Iran received a 15-point U.S. peace plan via Pakistan covering Strait of Hormuz reopening, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated Tehran is reviewing the U.S. peace plan but insists on no negotiations, while demanding an end to all fighting and attack guarantees.
Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf denied any U.S. negotiations on social media.
Iran released 20 cargo ships (mostly Iranian) through the Strait of Hormuz, which Trump framed as a 'present' and evidence of negotiation progress.
Pakistan, Egypt, and Turkey have served as intermediaries conveying U.S. messages to Iran, with potential talks planned for Pakistan this weekend.
Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz has spiked U.S. gas prices over $4 per gallon, slowed hiring, and raised recession fears.
Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz would take approximately two months to resume after a ceasefire according to estimates.
Trump threatened to bomb Iran 'back to the stone ages' and considered seizing Kharg Island oil or extracting Iran's enriched uranium.
U.S. strikes hit Iranian-backed Iraqi militia targets Monday, including an intelligence headquarters and a base near Haliwa Airport.
Israel warned Iranians not to use trains, signaling potential strikes on railway networks.
The U.S. military conducted a complex F-15 rescue operation in Iran on Friday, described by Trump as one of the military's most complex missions.
The U.S. deployed 2,000 additional Marines to the Middle East by Friday in response to ongoing conflict.
Israeli Defense Minister called the strikes that killed IRGC intelligence chief Khademi 'a major blow to Iran's war effort'.
Iran accumulated 221 wounded and 13 killed across nationalities in Gulf states and Israel from retaliatory fire since the war began.
Trump said Iran is negotiating 'in good faith' but insisted on free oil traffic through the Strait of Hormuz as a condition.
The war between the U.S.-Israel and Iran began on February 28, 2026, and entered its fourth week as of early April.
Trump expects the war to end 'very soon' or within weeks, claiming 'overwhelming victories' for the U.S.-Israel side.
An Iranian armed forces spokesperson warned adversaries are unsafe in global 'tourist centers'.

regime_fall.current_status: Day 42 sees intensifying military escalation with Israeli strikes on Tehran infrastructure and IRGC sites on April 8, while Iran continues regional attacks and vows 'Operation Crushing Revenge' for IRGC intelligence chief Khademi's death. Simultaneously, contradictory diplomatic signals emerge with Trump claiming good-faith negotiations, Iran receiving a 15-point U.S. peace plan, but Iranian leadership publicly denying negotiations while demanding permanent conflict resolution, amid hardline succession consolidation under Mojtaba Khamenei and hardline security appointments.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Israeli strikes hit Tehran early April 8 targeting IRGC sites, missile facilities, and petrochemical plants near Azadi Square and Sharif University, concurrent with U.S. deadline threats and Trump's claim of Iranian good-faith negotiations despite public Iranian denials.
